name: Bar Calders
fact: vermouth amb sifon per 2.80€, taules a fora fins tard

name: El Xampanyet
fact: house cava por 2.20€, llevan aquí desde los 30

name: Bar Marsella
fact: oldest bar in Barcelona, absinthe from 1820, dust on the bottles is real

name: Morro Fi
fact: natural wine, standing only, tiny place on Riera Alta

name: Bar Brutal
fact: natural wine, small plates, by the people who do Can Cesc

name: La Pepita
fact: montaditos por 1.50€, siempre lleno, imposible sentarse los fines de semana

name: Bar Leo
fact: bodega de barrio, vino peleón por 1.20€, solo locales

name: Bodega Sepúlveda
fact: vi de la casa a 1.50€, terrassa petita, veïns de tota la vida

name: Flash Flash
fact: tortillería desde 1970, interior retro blanco y negro, tortilla francesa de autor

name: Bar Olímpic
fact: vermut i patates, terrassa a la plaça del Sortidor, barri tranquil

name: Syra Coffee
fact: best specialty coffee in Gràcia, small batch roasters, no oat milk jokes

name: El 58
fact: brunch on weekends, locals pretend they don't know about it

name: Bar Canigó
fact: mercat de l'abaceria just a l'esquena, vermut amb olives, tables depuis 1922

name: La Cova Fumada
fact: invented the bombas, no menu, no sign, closes at 3pm, cash only

name: Nømad Coffee
fact: they roast their own, small space, usually a queue by 9am

name: Zum Schmutzigen Hobby
fact: weird taxidermy, Sternburg for 2€, no music before midnight

name: Luzia
fact: Oranienstrasse, Soviet posters, Club-Mate and cheap beer, always full

name: Monarch
fact: above Kotti supermarket, you go up a hidden staircase, drinks 3.50€

name: Würgeengel
fact: named after Buñuel film, velvet curtains, bartenders who ignore you the right amount

name: Five Elephant
fact: Cheesecake is the thing, specialty coffee, Reichenberger Str, always a queue weekend mornings

name: Café Müller
fact: named after Pina Bausch piece, regulars since 1960s, Schwarzwälder for 3.80€

name: Tante Lisbeth
fact: Weinbergswegstrasse, candlelight always, Pils 3.20€, opens at 7pm sharp

name: Kumpelnest 3000
fact: former brothel, Lützowstrasse, velvet and mirrors, open till 6am, regulars since 1984

name: Klunkerkranich
fact: rooftop above Neukölln Arcaden, 5€ entry, sunset from there is the best thing in Berlin

name: Standard Pizza
fact: Neapolitan, Mitte, lunch specials 8.50€, line out the door after 1pm

name: Roamers
fact: brunch spot, Pannierstrasse, avocado toast was cool here before it was everywhere else

name: Prater Garten
fact: oldest Biergarten in Berlin, under chestnut trees, Prater-Pils 4.20€

name: Aunt Benny
fact: tiny, Oderstrasse, filter coffee 2.50€, brunch queue starts at 10am Sundays

name: Hops & Barley
fact: microbrewery in a former butcher shop, Wühlischstrasse, house Pilsner 3.80€

name: Gordon's Wine Bar
fact: oldest wine bar in London, candles and newspaper clippings from 1890, house red £6

name: The French House
fact: half-pints only, no TV, no music, De Gaulle drank here during WW2

name: Bar Termini
fact: nine bar stools, negroni £12, modelled on a 1950s Roma train station bar

name: Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese
fact: rebuilt in 1667 after the Great Fire, sawdust on the floor, Dickens had a regular table

name: Swift
fact: two floors, upstairs is cocktails from £12, downstairs is Irish whiskey, staff actually know what they're doing

name: The Mayflower
fact: wooden deck over the Thames, named after the ship, pint of Timothy Taylor £5.20

name: Monmouth Coffee
fact: no wifi, no seats, just filter coffee in paper cups, queue round the corner every morning

name: Kiln
fact: Burmese clay pot cooking, counter seating only, no reservations, queue from 5:30pm

name: Blacklock City
fact: chops from £5 at lunch, all-in Sunday roast £26, hidden in a basement on Old Jewry

name: Brawn
fact: natural wine, Sunday lunch next to the flower market, Columbia Rd at its best

name: The Dove
fact: Thames riverside, narrowest bar room in UK, Fuller's London Pride £5.40

name: Cahoots
fact: inside a real 1940s underground station, cocktails in enamel cups, swing music

name: Brat
fact: Basque-inspired, wood fire, turbot for two £80, Michelin star they don't lean into

name: The Interval
fact: actually this is SF — skip

name: Milk Beach
fact: Australian specialty coffee, Bateman St, flat white £4.20, queue before 9am

name: Vesuvio
fact: Beat generation bar since 1948, Kerouac left his bag here once, house red $6

name: Smuggler's Cove
fact: 500+ rums, tiki cocktails, three floors, staff explain everything, somehow not annoying

name: Trick Dog
fact: cocktail menu changes every 6 months with a theme, 19th St, always ranked top 50 world

name: Zeitgeist
fact: huge beer garden, cash only, pitchers of Anchor Steam, motorcycles outside always

name: Tosca Cafe
fact: opera on the jukebox, house cappuccino has brandy in it, open since 1919

name: The Interval
fact: bar inside the Long Now Foundation, 10,000-year library behind the counter, cocktails $14

name: El Farolito
fact: best super burrito in SF, $11, 24h on weekends, line out the door after 2am

name: Tartine Bakery
fact: bread comes out at 5pm, line forms at 4:30, country loaf $14, worth it once

name: Sightglass Coffee
fact: industrial space, roast on-site, their Ethiopia is the one, 7th St, opens at 7am

name: Nopa
fact: wood-fired everything, open till 1am, communal tables, burger $24 but it's the burger

name: Foreign Cinema
fact: movies projected on the alley wall while you eat, oysters on brunch, 22nd St

name: Bi-Rite Creamery
fact: salted caramel soft serve, 18th St, line wraps around the block on any sunny day

name: The Monk's Kettle
fact: 150 beers on the list, gastropub before gastropub was a word, 16th St, cash tip appreciated

name: Comstock Saloon
fact: Victorian bar from 1907, original mahogany, pisco punch $14, jazz on Mondays

name: Casa Figari
fact: live jazz most nights, Carrer dels Mirallers, dark and cramped in the best way

name: Plaça de la Virreina
fact: neighbourhood square in Gràcia, outdoor cinema in summer evenings, Bar Virreina on the corner

name: Civil War Bomb Marks Building
fact: corner of Carrer de Calders — building still has visible bomb marks from the 1938 Civil War bombardment, almost nobody stops to look

name: Casa Almirall
fact: second oldest bar in Barcelona after Marsella, modernista interior from 1860, absinthe fountain still there

name: Paradiso
fact: pastrami sandwich counter in front is the door, cocktail bar behind the fridge, ranked world top 5 for years

name: Dry Martini
fact: Carrer d'Aribau, classic white-jacket bartenders, dry martini prepared tableside, open since 1978

name: Granja M. Viader
fact: invented Cacaolat in 1931, marble tables, white tiled walls, suís amb nata 2.80€

name: La Vinya del Senyor
fact: wine bar facing Santa Maria del Mar, terrace, 300 references, glass from 4€

name: Bar Electricitat
fact: oldest wine bar in Barcelona (1896), Carrer de Sant Carles, wine from the barrel, no fuss

name: Federal Café
fact: Australian-style brunch, Parlament street, smashed avo before it was everywhere

name: Heliogàbal
fact: basement venue on Carrer de Ramon i Cajal, poetry slams, jazz, experimental music, 5€ door

name: Jai-ca
fact: Carrer del Ginebró, best gambas al ajillo in Barceloneta, standing at the bar only, cash

name: Napar BCN
fact: craft beer + Michelin-starred pintxos kitchen, Carrer de la Diputació, brewing in the basement

name: El Bar del Convent
fact: inside a 15th century convent courtyard, slowest service in Barcelona, worth it

name: Mercat de Santa Caterina
fact: mosaic roof by Miralles, built on a medieval cemetery, cheaper and less touristic than Boqueria

name: Bar Olimpic
fact: on Plaça del Sortidor, old men playing dominoes in the morning, vermouth crowd takes over at noon

name: La Caseta del Migdia
fact: hidden on the back of Montjuïc, open Fri-Sun, best sunset in Barcelona, bring cash

name: Clärchens Ballhaus
fact: ballroom from 1913, tango and salsa nights, ceiling mirrors, schnitzel in the garden

name: Markthalle Neun
fact: Street Food Thursday every week, 200 vendors, 19th century market hall, Eisenbahnstrasse

name: Schwarze Traube
fact: natural wine bar, Wrangelstrasse, 8 seats only, no menu — they ask what you like

name: Becketts Kopf
fact: Samuel Beckett portrait in the window, no sign, ring the bell, cocktails from 13€

name: Café Sibylle
fact: Karl-Marx-Allee, DDR museum café, original 1950s interior, coffee 2.20€, locals hate tourists here

name: Club der Visionäre
fact: floating on a canal arm, techno Sunday afternoons, Flutgraben, Berghain's more gentle sibling

name: Silo Coffee
fact: Gabriel-Max-Strasse, single origin pourover, bagels, regulars sit at the same table every day

name: Wohnzimmer
fact: Lettestrasse, mismatched grandma furniture, Berliner Weisse 3€, board games on request

name: Dr. Pong
fact: one ping-pong table in the middle, you walk around the table, Fehrbelliner Str, no reservations for the table

name: Michelberger Hotel Bar
fact: creative hub bar on Warschauer Str, local artists, natural wine, Sunday brunch that runs all day

name: Rosenthaler Grill
fact: Torstrasse, döner at 3am and 3pm equally, the standard all other kebabs are judged by

name: Kimchi Princess
fact: Skalitzerstrasse, Korean BBQ you grill yourself, soju tower, open till 2am

name: E. Pellicci
fact: Italian caff since 1900, Grade II listed interior, full English £9.50, same family five generations

name: St. John
fact: Fergus Henderson's nose-to-tail restaurant in a former smokehouse, bone marrow on toast £12

name: The Anchor & Hope
fact: no reservations except Sunday lunch, gastropub, The Cut, turns people away every night

name: Leila's Shop
fact: Calvert Avenue, seasonal groceries and café, closes when the vegetables run out

name: Café OTO
fact: experimental music and jazz, Ashwin St, café by day, concerts by night, no bad acts

name: Lahore Kebab House
fact: Umberston St, cash only, lamb chops £12, open since 1976, still has a queue

name: Maltby Street Market
fact: under the railway arches, Saturday and Sunday mornings only, less known than Borough, better food

name: Quality Chop House
fact: Victorian working men's dining room, original bench seating, confit potatoes are the thing

name: Broadway Market
fact: Saturday market, London Fields end, organic bread and vintage, the unofficial heart of E8

name: Berber & Q
fact: Middle Eastern BBQ under the arches, Acton Mews, smoky and loud, no reservations

name: Sessions Arts Club
fact: 1779 Georgian courthouse, ceiling frescoes, natural wine list, feels like a secret

name: Nightjar
fact: speakeasy, prohibition-era cocktails, City Road, live jazz, reservation required

name: The Barbican Lakeside
fact: brutalist architecture café on the water, pre-concert cheese boards, locals pretend they live there

name: City Lights Books
fact: Ferlinghetti's bookshop since 1953, open till midnight, Beat poetry basement, Columbus Ave

name: Amoeba Music
fact: Haight St, 100,000 used records, listening stations, cash for your collection

name: The Mill
fact: Josey Baker bread, avocado toast that started the meme, Divisadero St, opens at 7am

name: Dandelion Chocolate
fact: bean-to-bar factory and café, Valencia St, single-origin bars, hot chocolate is the reason to come

name: La Palma Mexicatessen
fact: 24th St, handmade tortillas since 1953, you can watch them make them through the window

name: Spec's
fact: Columbus Ave, dive bar covered floor-to-ceiling in sailor memorabilia, cash only, open since 1968

name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
fact: Valencia St, started the SF third-wave coffee thing, single-origin pourover, no laptops at counter

name: The Roxie Theater
fact: oldest continuously operating cinema in SF (1909), 16th St, rep programming, 3-film marathons

name: Zuni Café
fact: Caesar salad invented here in 1987, roast chicken for two in a wood-fired oven $78, open since 1979

name: Tony's Pizza Napoletana
fact: Stockton St, Tony Gemignani won World Pizza Championship 12 times, queue starts before opening

name: The Fillmore
fact: Fillmore St, Hendrix and Grateful Dead played here, free apple at the door, posters collected by everyone

name: Café du Nord
fact: basement venue under Swedish American Hall, Market St, intimate shows, 1907 bar

name: Liholiho Yacht Club
fact: Hawaiian-influenced, no reservations, walk-ins only, Polk St, lines start at 5pm
